title: TLS and UDP Transport
text:
[REQ-0004] The agent MUST listen for inbound SNMP connections over TLS as specified in RFC 6353.
[REQ-0005] The agent MUST bind a TCP socket on port 10161 (the IANA-assigned SNMP-over-TLS port) by default.
[REQ-0006] It MUST accept a configuration parameter to override the listen address and port.
[REQ-0007] The agent MUST use the TLS message framing defined in RFC 6353 §5 (length-prefixed SNMP messages over the TLS record layer).

[REQ-0008] The agent MUST send outbound trap PDUs over plain UDP with no encryption or authentication.
[REQ-0009] The default destination port for traps MUST be 162.

[REQ-0010] The agent MUST support SNMPv3 only.
[REQ-0011] After TLS decryption, the agent MUST silently discard any payload that does not decode as a valid SNMPv3 message.
[REQ-0012] Trap PDUs MUST also be valid SNMPv3 messages.

[REQ-0013] The agent MUST support IPv4 and IPv6.

[REQ-0014] The initial implementation SHOULD use the `rustls` crate as the TLS engine.

**Rationale:**
RFC 6353 defines SNMP over TLS, providing confidentiality and certificate-based peer authentication for inbound requests.
Plain UDP for traps avoids the complexity of maintaining TLS connections to trap destinations and is sufficient for unacknowledged notifications.
Port 10161 is the IANA-assigned port for SNMP over TLS.
